mechanism_narrative: The prevailing federal funds rate directly determines the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows; a higher FEDFUNDS level reduces the present value of future corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.0% signals excess labor market slack that erodes wage growth and consumer spending power, leading to downward corporate earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and industrial production reflects broad demand-supply expansion that drives aggregate revenue growth beyond what either indicator shows in isolation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and INDPRO does not significantly predict positive 20-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is in a tightening cycle with the funds rate above 2.5%, accelerating consumer price inflation causes markets to reprice terminal rate expectations higher, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in less than 45% of instances or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts translate into higher residential investment and construction employment, which boosts aggregate demand and generates positive earnings momentum for the broad index over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns fails to materialize with Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward regression of INDPRO growth on 5-day forward returns yields a negative coefficient significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A negative three-month change in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting labor income that reduces aggregate consumption expenditures, triggering negative earnings surprises and negative S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: Negative 3-month change in PAYEMS coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captures the economy's aggregate demand trajectory; positive GDPC1 growth supports broad-based revenue expansion across the index, producing positive S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to positively correlate with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window (Spearman rho <= 0).
